task_completion.description: A title page is created with the correct title and subtitle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first page of the document should clearly display the title 'NetWatch Pro v3.2' and subtitle 'Administrator Manual', typically centered and in a large font.

task_completion.description: A Table of Contents is included in the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Table of Contents section is visible, listing the document's headings (e.g., Introduction, System Requirements) with corresponding page numbers.

task_completion.description: Hierarchical heading styles (H1, H2) are applied to the document sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Main sections like 'System Requirements' and 'Configuration' are formatted as large H1 headings. Subsections like 'Hardware Requirements' and 'Global Configuration' are formatted as H2 headings (slightly smaller than H1 but distinct from body text).

task_completion.description: System requirements, configuration parameters, and error codes are formatted into tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for actual word processor table grids (with rows, columns, and cell borders) containing keywords like 'CPU', 'RAM', 'nw.scan.interval', 'nw.alert.threshold', 'E001', or 'E002'.

task_completion.description: Command-line examples are formatted using a monospace font.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Commands such as 'netwatch --discover', 'netwatch --monitor', 'nw-config --set', and 'nw-service restart' appear in a distinct monospace (typewriter-style) font.

task_completion.description: The formatted document is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word processor's title bar or a save dialog shows the file name as 'netwatch_manual.odt', indicating the changes have been saved to the required file.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical word processor (like Calligra Words or LibreOffice Writer) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GUI of a word processing application with formatting toolbars, not just a terminal window or a plain text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used genuine word processor features for tables and TOC, rather than manually typing them out with spaces or ASCII characters.
integrity.visual_evidence: Tables should have actual cell boundaries/gridlines provided by the word processor. The TOC should look like a generated field, not manually typed text with spaces or dots.

integrity.description: The agent modified the target .odt file rather than creating a new markdown or text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window should clearly indicate it is editing an .odt file, not an .md or .txt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is '/home/ga/Documents/netwatch_manual.odt'. The title must be 'NetWatch Pro v3.2' and the subtitle 'Administrator Manual'. Expected H1 sections include: Introduction, System Requirements, Installation, Configuration, Command Reference, Troubleshooting, API Reference, Appendix. Expected H2 subsections include: Hardware Requirements, Software Requirements, Linux Installation, Windows Installation, Global Configuration, Alert Configuration, Network Discovery Commands, Monitoring Commands, Common Error Codes, Performance Tuning. Tables must contain keywords like CPU, RAM, Disk, nw.scan.interval, nw.alert.threshold, E001, E002. Monospace formatting must be applied to commands: 'netwatch --discover', 'netwatch --monitor', 'nw-config --set', 'nw-service restart'.
